title: malpighia subpilosa
intro: Malpighia subpilosa, also known as the wild guava or Panama apple, is a species of flowering plant in the Malpighiaceae family, native to Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Malpighia subpilosa include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. Malpighia subpilosa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
